WeBox CEO's Letter of Q1, 2026
This email contains confidential information. No circulation without written permission.
To Our Valued Shareholders and the WeBox Team,
Last year, I shared a simple but important message with the team: We need drivers, not passengers, at WeBox.
Today, I want to bring that message to life by sharing several real stories from our team—stories that reflect who we are, what we 
value, and how we win.
Result-Driven
Last Friday, I had lunch with Martin. During our conversation, he shared something that genuinely surprised me.
Martin told me that Anthony often says he doesn’t want to waste a single minute when he’s out doing field prospecting. His goal 
is always to move conversations forward and secure demos. Martin even said he wished he had “another Anthony” in San 
Francisco to help accelerate revenue growth.
What surprised me wasn’t Anthony’s performance—I already knew he was strong. What surprised me was hearing, for the first 
time, someone from the team explicitly articulate “not wasting time” as a core personal principle. I’ve repeated many times that 
we should never waste our own time or the company’s time—but hearing it reflected back organically from the team mattered.
When I returned to the office and reviewed Anthony’s OKRs over the past three quarters, one thing was clear: he consistently 
exceeded expectations.
Last December, we were struggling to close the Dyna Robotics deal. Without changes, the deal would have slipped into the new 
year. We reassigned the account to Anthony. The CEO of Dyna Robotics quickly developed strong trust in him and later told us 
directly that Anthony helped accelerate the deal by nearly one full month.
What makes this story more meaningful is that Anthony was not a typical “sales hire” on paper. When we hired him, he was 
nervous, introverted, lacked polished sales techni...(truncated)
Now let me extract the previous quarterly letters to compare with Q1 2026:

--- C:\Coding Projects\resume screening\e2e_test_workspace\resources\WeBox CEO's Letter of Q4, 2025.pdf ---
WeBox CEO's Letter of Q4, 2025​
This email contains confidential information. No circulation without written permission.​
To our valued shareholders and WeBox team,​
Overall, Q3 was another fantastic quarter. Thank you for your continued support throughout this 
journey. I hope you find these updates helpful. As always, feel free to reach out at any time for a 
conversation.​
Fred Ming​
CEO, WeBox Inc​
Executive Summary​
•
Revenue​
Q3 Total Revenue: WeBox achieved total revenue of $6.66M [Forecast: $6.56M]. This marks an 
18.29% QoQ growth from $5.63M in Q2 2025 and represent a 90.83% YoY increase from $3.46M in 
Q3 2024. ​
YTD Total Revenue: $17M.​
•
Gross Margin​
Q3 Gross Margin: Gross margin was 28.47% [Forecast: 28.65%], down 0.06% QoQ from 30.17% in 
Q2 2025, and down 0.01% YoY from 28.86% in Q3 2024. [We adopted a slightly more aggressive 
pricing strategy to attract new customers.]​
•
Net Profit​
Q3 Net Profit: –$332K [Forecast: $20K], driven by higher costs from:​
•
Legal (+70K vs Q2)​

•
Payroll (+207K vs Q2)​
•
upgrading our San Carlos and SF locations (+88K vs Q2)​
•
Fraud (+49K)​
•
Fremont Kitchen​
Signed 18 agreements and only 3 workstations are available.​
Highlights of Business​
•
WeBox Mart​
Quarterly revenue reached nearly $400K, with Q3 totaling $389,771 — a 38% increase compared 
to Q2’s $282,384. We also expanded our product offerings with ready-to-eat meals, which 
significantly improved our gross margin.​
•
Sunnyvale hub is open​
The Sunnyvale hub opened in September, helping resolve late delivery issues in the Palo Alto 
and Mountain View areas. It also redistributes part of the revenue from the San Jose hub, 
allowing San Jose to handle more orders within its local area.​
•
R&D reorganized under three product owners​
This restructuring improves iteration velocity, enhances customer proximity, and minimizes 
reliance on any single produc...(truncated)

args.name: WeBox_Q1_2026_Update.md
args.content:
# WeBox Q1 2026 Monitoring Update

## Executive Summary

Q1 2026 represents a pivotal transition period for WeBox, with clear progress toward profitability and active Series B fundraising. The company has maintained strong revenue growth while addressing previous financial concerns.

---

## Financial Performance Comparison

### 2025 Full Year Results (from Q1 2026 Letter)
| Metric | Actual | Forecast | Variance |
|--------|--------|----------|----------|
| Revenue | $23.65M | $24M | -1.5% |
| Net Profit | -$658K | $500K | **-$1.158M miss** |
| Gross Margin | 30.43% | 30% | +0.43% |

### Quarterly Trend Analysis

| Quarter | Revenue | QoQ Growth | YoY Growth | Net Profit | Gross Margin |
|---------|---------|------------|------------|------------|--------------|
| Q1 2025 | ~$4.67M* | - | ~105% | -$116K | 30.37% |
| Q2 2025 | $5.63M | +20.48% | +105% | -$46K | 30.17% |
| Q3 2025 | $6.66M | +18.29% | +90.83% | -$332K | 28.47% |
| Q4 2025 | $6.60M | -0.9% | +68% | -$246K | 31.72% |
| **Q1 2026 Goal** | **$7.9M** | **+19.69%** | **-** | **-$50K** | **32%** |

*Estimated from Q2 data

### Key Observations:
1. **Q4 2025 Recovery**: Net profit improved 25.9% QoQ from Q3's -$332K to -$246K, showing cost control improvements
2. **Margin Rebound**: Q4 gross margin jumped to 31.72% from Q3's 28.47%, reversing the margin compression trend
3. **2025 Full Year**: Despite Q3 miss, full year net loss narrowed 8.4% YoY (-$658K vs -$719K in 2024)

---

## 2026 Targets & Path to Profitability

### Annual 2026 Goals
| Metric | Target | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Revenue | $45M | +90.27% YoY from $23.65M |
| Gross Margin | 31% | Slight decline from Q4 2025's 31.72% |
| Net Profit | **$100K** | **First profitable quarter expected!** |

### New Revenue Streams (2026 Goals)
| Business Line | Target Revenue | Status |
|---------------|----------------|--------|
| TPT - Event Catering | $4M | Q4 2025: $217K (+170% QoQ) |
| TPT - Self-Pay Lunch | $2M | Q4 2025: $377K (+85.7% YoY) |
| Office Pantry | N/A | Q4 2025: $31K (-15.2% QoQ) |

**Assessment**: New revenue streams show strong momentum but need significant scaling to meet 2026 targets.

---

## Fundraising Progress - Major Update

### Series B Status (from Pitch Deck v46)
- **Target Raise**: $15M
- **Use of Funds**:
  - 30% - Sales & Marketing
  - 20% - Product & Engineering
  - 20% - Operations & Hub Expansion
  - 30% - New Demand Verticals

### Timeline Comparison
| Period | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Q2 2025 | Series B officially kicked off (June 2025) |
| Q3 2025 | Due diligence with John Paulson Banking & Alloy Technology Partners; Capstone Partners upcoming |
| Q4 2025 | No specific update in letter |
| **Q1 2026** | **Pitch deck shows active fundraising - $15M Series B target** |

**Assessment**: Fundraising appears to be in advanced stages based on pitch deck distribution. No mention of deal closure yet.

---

## Operational Metrics

### Headcount Evolution
| Period | Total | US | China | Philippines |
|--------|-------|-----|-------|-------------|
| Q2 2025 | 91 | 36 | 31 | 24 |
| Q3 2025 | 94 | 34 | 33 | 27 |
| Q4 2025 | 99 | 35 | 33 | 33 |

**Growth**: +8 employees since Q2 2025, steady expansion

### Hub Expansion
- **Current**: SF Bay Area (SF, San Carlos, Mountain View, Sunnyvale, Fremont Kitchen)
- **2026 Plan**: Expand to Los Angeles (1H 2026), then San Diego & Seattle (1H 2027)
- **Target**: 17+ hubs across West Coast by end of 2026

---

## Strategic Initiatives

### AI-Native Transformation (3-Year Goal)
| Initiative | Target | Current Status |
|------------|--------|----------------|
| AI-implemented software products | 80% | In progress |
| AI-powered task workflows | 20% | Pilot phase |
| Task-based (not role-based) workflows | Launch v1 | Development |

**Note**: Company acknowledges being "behind Silicon Valley tech companies" in AI adoption but making concerted effort.

### Talent Philosophy Changes
- Eliminated traditional performance evaluations
- Focus on OKRs, 360-degree feedback, retention tests
- Pay top-of-market compensation
- Invest disproportionately in top 20%
- Reject mediocrity - retention alone not goal

**Recent Leadership Change**: Technical team restructuring - recruiting director-level leader with AI expertise, replacing Luke after open conversations

---

## Positive Signals

1. **Profitability Path**: Clear roadmap to $100K net profit in 2026, first time seeing positive earnings target
2. **Margin Recovery**: Q4 2025 gross margin rebounded to 31.72%, addressing Q3 compression concerns
3. **New Revenue Momentum**: Event Catering +170% QoQ, WeBox Mart +85.7% YoY in Q4 2025
4. **Customer Retention**: Strong testimonials from Weston, Chris indicating employee satisfaction
5. **LTV/CAC Efficiency**: Pitch deck shows 17x ratio - excellent unit economics
6. **Hub Utilization**: Fremont Kitchen at 18/21 workstations signed (~86%)

---

## Red Flags / Areas of Concern

1. **2025 Net Profit Miss**: Full year -$658K vs $500K forecast = **$1.158M variance** - significant concern
2. **Q3 2025 Loss Spike**: -$332K driven by:
   - Legal costs +$70K
   - Payroll +$207K
   - Location upgrades +$88K
   - Fraud +$49K
3. **Revenue Growth Slowdown**: Q4 2025 showed -0.9% QoQ decline, breaking the consistent ~20% quarterly growth pattern
4. **Office Pantry Decline**: -15.2% QoQ in Q4 2025 - one revenue stream contracting
5. **Leadership Turnover**: Technical leadership change raises questions about execution capability
6. **Fundraising Timeline**: No concrete closing date despite advanced due diligence mentioned in Q3

---

## Competitive Landscape

From Pitch Deck:
- **No direct competitors** offering WeBox's logistics infrastructure, breadth, cost advantages, and ease of use
- **Differentiation**: Hub-and-spoke model, 2000+ daily selections, 20% lower prices, AI-powered optimization
- **Market Position**: B2B2C disruptor in last-mile food delivery

**Note**: Previous monitoring noted competitor lawsuit (validation of value proposition) - no update in Q1 2026 materials

---

## Investment Recommendation Considerations

### Strengths
- ✓ Strong revenue growth trajectory (90% YoY in 2025)
- ✓ Clear path to profitability in 2026
- ✓ Excellent LTV/CAC ratio (17x)
- ✓ Diversifying revenue streams successfully
- ✓ Active Series B fundraising with defined use of funds

### Risks
- ⚠️ Significant 2025 profitability miss
- ⚠️ Q4 2025 revenue stagnation (-0.9% QoQ)
- ⚠️ Ongoing legal exposure
- ⚠️ Execution risk on aggressive 2026 targets ($45M revenue = +90% YoY)
- ⚠️ Fundraising timeline uncertainty

### Key Questions for Management
1. What caused the $1.158M net profit miss in 2025?
2. What drove Q4 2025 revenue flatline after consistent 20% growth?
3. Status of Series B - which investors are committed?
4. How is fraud prevention being addressed after $49K loss in Q3?
5. Timeline for LA expansion and new hub openings?

---

## Data Sources
- WeBox CEO's Letter of Q1, 2026.pdf
- WeBox Pitch Deck v46.pdf
- Historical CEO Letters Q2-Q4 2025 (for comparison)

---

*Last Updated: Q1 2026 Review*
*Next Review: Q2 2026 CEO Letter (expected April/May 2026)*
